name: Ethel's Grill
tagline: Tiny Kalihi gem where the garlic ahi and oxtail soup are worth the early wake-up.
mustOrder: Garlic ahi steak — seared rare with garlic butter, the signature dish
Oxtail soup — rich, fall-off-the-bone, with mustard cabbage
Shoyu pork — tender braised pork belly in sweet shoyu
parking: Small lot in front of the strip mall. Gets packed during lunch — arrive by 10:30am.
talkStory: Ethel's has been in this unassuming Kalihi strip mall forever, run by the same family. Lunch is the move — they open at 6am for breakfast but the hot stuff starts rolling at 10. The garlic ahi is legitimately one of the best things you'll eat on this island for under $15. Portions are local-style generous. This is deep Kalihi — not a tourist in sight. Cash preferred but they do take cards now.
